ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sites" (
  "site_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each site */,
  "site_name" TEXT /* Name of the site */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the site */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the site */,
  "latitude" TEXT /* Site's geographical latitude */,
  "longitude" TEXT /* Site's geographical longitude */,
  "elevation" TEXT /* Site's elevation above sea level */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Site's timezone */,
  "description" TEXT /* Site's description or notes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("site_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensors" (
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor */,
  "site_id" INTEGER /* ID of the site where the sensor is deployed */,
  "sensor_type" TEXT /* Type of sensor (e.g., temperature, humidity) */,
  "sensor_model" TEXT /* Model of the sensor */,
  "calibration_info" TEXT /* Sensor's calibration information */,
  "maintenance_schedule" TEXT /* Sensor's maintenance schedule and history */,
  "measurement_units" TEXT /* Sensor's measurement units */,
  "transmission_protocol" TEXT /* Sensor's data transmission protocol */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensors_site_id FOREIGN KEY ("site_id") REFERENCES sites ("site_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensors_sensor_type FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_type") REFERENCES sensor_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "readings" (
  "reading_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reading */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor that took the reading */,
  "reading_date" TEXT /* Date the reading was taken */,
  "reading_value" REAL /* Value of the reading */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the reading with millisecond precision */,
  "quality_control" TEXT /* Reading's quality control flags */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Reading's notes or comments */,
  "average_value" REAL /* Reading's average value (for aggregated data) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reading_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_readings_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the sensor type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sensor type */,
  "measurement_range" TEXT /* Sensor type's measurement range */,
  "accuracy" TEXT /* Sensor type's accuracy specifications */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* User's password (hashed for security) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* User's role's permissions and access levels */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* User's last login date and time */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "reading_id" INTEGER /* ID of the reading being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the reading */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the reading was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* Access event's IP address */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* Access event's user agent information */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Access event's notes or comments */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_reading_id FOREIGN KEY ("reading_id") REFERENCES readings ("reading_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
column_names: Contact email for inquiries about the site
ID of the site where the sensor is deployed
ID of the sensor that took the reading
Timestamp of the reading with millisecond precision
Unique identifier for each sensor type
Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin)
